Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

Andreas – It looks like the test 160a failure is legitimate; https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sets/46b115e8-8460-11e7-b90b-5254006e85c2. 
I’ve reproduced manually what sanity test 160a does; first the call to test_mkdir

# mkdir -p /lustre/scratch/d160a.sanity/pics/2008
[root@eagle-55vm6 ~]# lfs changelog scratch-MDT0000
95 02MKDIR 23:53:47.813205172 2017.08.27 0x0 t=[0x200000bd2:0x20:0x0] j=mkdir.0 p=[0x200000007:0x1:0x0] d160a.sanity
96 02MKDIR 23:53:47.819063456 2017.08.27 0x0 t=[0x200000bd2:0x21:0x0] j=mkdir.0 p=[0x200000bd2:0x20:0x0] pics
97 02MKDIR 23:53:47.823557192 2017.08.27 0x0 t=[0x200000bd2:0x22:0x0] j=mkdir.0 p=[0x200000bd2:0x21:0x0] 2008
[root@eagle-55vm6 ~]# lfs changelog scratch-MDT0001

# lfs setdirstripe -i1 -c2 /lustre/scratch/d160a.sanity/pics/2008/zachy
 [root@eagle-55vm6 ~]# lfs changelog scratch-MDT0000
95 02MKDIR 23:53:47.813205172 2017.08.27 0x0 t=[0x200000bd2:0x20:0x0] j=mkdir.0 p=[0x200000007:0x1:0x0] d160a.sanity
96 02MKDIR 23:53:47.819063456 2017.08.27 0x0 t=[0x200000bd2:0x21:0x0] j=mkdir.0 p=[0x200000bd2:0x20:0x0] pics
97 02MKDIR 23:53:47.823557192 2017.08.27 0x0 t=[0x200000bd2:0x22:0x0] j=mkdir.0 p=[0x200000bd2:0x21:0x0] 2008
[root@eagle-55vm6 ~]# lfs changelog scratch-MDT0001
31 02MKDIR 23:55:09.675564893 2017.08.27 0x0 t=[0x240000bd2:0xb:0x0] j=lfs.0 p=[0x200000bd2:0x22:0x0] zachy

Now, let’s ‘touch’ timestamp, which should have the parent (zachy) FID of [0x240000bd2:0xb:0x0]:

# touch /lustre/scratch/d160a.sanity/pics/2008/zachy/timestamp
# lfs changelog scratch-MDT0000
95 02MKDIR 23:53:47.813205172 2017.08.27 0x0 t=[0x200000bd2:0x20:0x0] j=mkdir.0 p=[0x200000007:0x1:0x0] d160a.sanity
96 02MKDIR 23:53:47.819063456 2017.08.27 0x0 t=[0x200000bd2:0x21:0x0] j=mkdir.0 p=[0x200000bd2:0x20:0x0] pics
97 02MKDIR 23:53:47.823557192 2017.08.27 0x0 t=[0x200000bd2:0x22:0x0] j=mkdir.0 p=[0x200000bd2:0x21:0x0] 2008
98 01CREAT 23:56:43.289385118 2017.08.27 0x0 t=[0x200000bd2:0x23:0x0] j=touch.0 p=[0x2000013a1:0x3:0x0] timestamp
99 11CLOSE 23:56:43.294380244 2017.08.27 0x42 t=[0x200000bd2:0x23:0x0] j=touch.0
# lfs changelog scratch-MDT0001
31 02MKDIR 23:55:09.675564893 2017.08.27 0x0 t=[0x240000bd2:0xb:0x0] j=lfs.0 p=[0x200000bd2:0x22:0x0] zachy

# lfs path2fid /lustre/scratch/d160a.sanity/pics/zach
[0x240000bd2:0xb:0x0]

As you can see, timestamp has parent FID [0x2000013a1:0x3:0x0]. I don’t understand where ‘timestamp’ parent FID is coming from.

All files created in /lustre/…/zachy have the parent FID [0x2000013a1:0x3:0x0]; see the parent to pic1.jpg:
# cp /etc/hosts /lustre/scratch/d160a.sanity/pics/2008/zachy/pic1.jpg
# lfs changelog scratch-MDT0000
95 02MKDIR 23:53:47.813205172 2017.08.27 0x0 t=[0x200000bd2:0x20:0x0] j=mkdir.0 p=[0x200000007:0x1:0x0] d160a.sanity
96 02MKDIR 23:53:47.819063456 2017.08.27 0x0 t=[0x200000bd2:0x21:0x0] j=mkdir.0 p=[0x200000bd2:0x20:0x0] pics
97 02MKDIR 23:53:47.823557192 2017.08.27 0x0 t=[0x200000bd2:0x22:0x0] j=mkdir.0 p=[0x200000bd2:0x21:0x0] 2008
98 01CREAT 23:56:43.289385118 2017.08.27 0x0 t=[0x200000bd2:0x23:0x0] j=touch.0 p=[0x2000013a1:0x3:0x0] timestamp
99 11CLOSE 23:56:43.294380244 2017.08.27 0x42 t=[0x200000bd2:0x23:0x0] j=touch.0
100 01CREAT 23:58:04.531106145 2017.08.27 0x0 t=[0x200000bd2:0x24:0x0] j=cp.0 p=[0x2000013a1:0x3:0x0] pic1.jpg
101 11CLOSE 23:58:04.539836943 2017.08.27 0xc2 t=[0x200000bd2:0x24:0x0] j=cp.0
[root@eagle-55vm6 ~]# lfs changelog scratch-MDT0001
31 02MKDIR 23:55:09.675564893 2017.08.27 0x0 t=[0x240000bd2:0xb:0x0] j=lfs.0 p=[0x200000bd2:0x22:0x0] zachy

I’m guessing that, since the files are created on MDT0 and their parent is on MDT1, that the FIDs don’t match up. Is this expected?What do you think?

parents.subject: LU-8912 nodemap: fix contiguous range support

commit.subject: LU-9624 tests: fix pre-DNE test exceptions/llog usage
commit.message:
LU-9624 tests: fix pre-DNE test exceptions/llog usage

Remove some test skips when running with multiple MDTs in DNE mode,
or fix tests to work better with multiple MDTs.  Tests updated are:
recovery-small: 60
sanity: 17hi, 154ab, 160abcde, 161abcd, 162a, 205, 225ab, 254, 256

In particular, sanity.sh test_160, test_161, test_162 ignored test
failures in DNE mode.  Fix test_160* to work with ChangeLogs stored
on multiple MDTs.  This adds test coverage both because we aren't
skipping these tests when running in DNE mode, but also because we
are now validating ChangeLogs running on multiple MDTs at once.

Test-Parameters: trivial mdscount=2 mdtcount=4 testlist=sanity,recovery-small,sanity-hsm
